reasoning: B is the most rigorous and best-calibrated: it names the question-substitution trap up front, applies Meadows' leverage hierarchy consistently, surfaces the landlord bounded-rationality equilibrium as the real lever, attaches an explicit ~80% confidence to the diagnosis vs. lower confidence on remedies, red-teams Option B, and lists the specific unknowns (ownership concentration, fiscal room) that determine which lever works. C shares B's residency reframe and adds genuinely non-obvious 'measurement artifact' and 'coordination failure' framings with control-block test designs, but quantifies uncertainty less and probes incentives less deeply. A has the most vivid reframings (habit problem, frictionless life, vacancy-as-asset) and concrete trip-count test designs, but its Fermi numbers are presented without flagging them as rough estimates and it engages least with its own cognitive traps and alternative explanations.
